authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1200 - Hazard Communication
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for workplace labeling, shipped-container labeling, SDS content, and employer hazard communication duties in U.S. chemical operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Communication: Small Entity Compliance Guide for Employers That Use Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official compliance guide is authoritative public workflow guidance for written programs, container-label checks, SDS maintenance, training, and periodic reassessment in real plant settings.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Communication - Questions and Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official FAQ set is the public interpretive baseline for update timing, workplace-label edge cases, and shipped-container obligations that often drive labeling escalations.
used_for: low-confidence handling when facts are incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Inspection Procedures for the Hazard Communication Standard (HCS 2012)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's enforcement directive is the authoritative public inspection workflow reference for how labels, SDSs, written programs, and training records are gathered and reviewed during enforcement activity.

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 172 Subpart E - Labeling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for hazardous-material transport label requirements once a chemical package enters transportation handling.
used_for: shipment hold decisions tied to missing transport labels

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 172 Subpart H - Training
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for hazmat employee training and recordkeeping that gates shipment-prep activity tied to chemical labels.

authoritative_sources.title: DOT CHART 17 Hazardous Materials Markings, Labeling and Placarding Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's official guide is an authoritative public workflow reference for label types, transport-facing terminology, and field checks used by warehouse and shipping teams.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: field verification and warehouse release review

authoritative_sources.title: How to Use the HMR
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's official training reference is authoritative for determining when hazardous-material transportation rules apply and which HMR sections control the labeling workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: scope triage and transport applicability review
used_for: handoff boundaries to transport compliance owners

authoritative_sources.title: Hazmat Transportation Training Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's official training reference clarifies required training content and recurring review cadence for hazmat employees whose work includes label-dependent shipping activity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: training evidence and shipment-release blocker review

authoritative_sources.title: Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS), Rev. 10
authoritative_sources.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The UN GHS is the recognized chemical-labeling standard for hazard classes, pictograms, statements, and SDS terminology that underpins modern public labeling frameworks.

workflow_stages.name: request intake and scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify jurisdiction, product family, formulation status, container type, shipment state, and whether the request remains inside chemical-labeling process support before making any recommendation.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the chemical, package, or workplace use case to the exact label, SDS, training, revision-control, and transport-overlap obligations required before packaging, shipment, or relabel activity can proceed.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and consistency review
workflow_stages.description: Collect and compare SDS records, hazard classification outputs, label text, artwork proofs, print controls, training records, batch and lot traceability, and shipping documents needed to support a bounded disposition.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: inspection, field-condition, and shipment-readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible label condition, inspection findings, transport label checks, and site observations support release recommendation, hold posture, or remediation packaging without implying final authority.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception, changed-information, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conflicting sources, changed hazard information, damaged labels, product-claim requests, legal sufficiency questions, and engineering or safety-critical decisions to the correct owners.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

artifacts: hazard-classification worksheets and constituent data records
SDS authoring files and approved SDS revisions
shipped-container labels, workplace labels, artwork proofs, and print records
batch tickets, lot traceability records, packaging specifications, and quarantine or relabel logs
shipping papers, hazmat descriptions, transport-label checks, and warehouse release checklists
training records, inspection reports, CAPA notes, incident logs, and damaged-label observations
systems_of_record: product lifecycle management or formulation repository
document control or quality management system

allowed: classify chemical-labeling requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, label-readiness summaries, inspection-response notes, and remediation packets tied to named artifacts and owners
map OSHA, SDS, workplace-label, and DOT transport-overlap obligations to review cadence and exception owners without improvising formal authority
escalate: required tenant facts, formulation status, shipment state, or source-of-truth mapping are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, jurisdictional, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether a label or shipment package can proceed
changed hazard information, damaged labels, release pressure, or field conditions increase environmental or safety risk
requests for legal sufficiency, product-claim approval, engineering judgment, toxicology interpretation, transport classification, or regulator-facing response
refuse: requests for final legal signoff, guaranteed regulator acceptance, or final market-label approval
requests to proceed with shipment, packaging, or return to service when required label, SDS, training, or inspection evidence is missing or contradictory
requests that collapse the lane into generic document review and ignore chemical-specific labeling, transport, or field-risk context

domain_failure_modes: Treating a workplace label and a shipped-container or DOT transport label as interchangeable without confirming the actual package and shipment state.
Allowing packaging or shipment to proceed even though SDS revisions, hazard statements, pictograms, or training records are stale, missing, or contradictory.
Treating damaged or missing labels in the field as minor formatting defects instead of answer-changing safety and compliance events.
Collapsing regulatory labeling into product marketing, customer claims, toxicology, or legal interpretation work that belongs to adjacent specialists.
Failing to preserve revision control when formulation, supplier, or hazard information changes but legacy labels remain in circulation.
Producing a label-readiness conclusion without named systems of record, evidence owners, or artifact traceability across EHS, PLM, ERP, and shipping systems.
Ignoring inspection or transport-overlap evidence and giving a generic compliance answer that is not usable by plant, warehouse, or EHS operators.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-U.S. market labeling and customer-specific label overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline here covers U.S. workplace and transport workflows. Export-market and customer-specific label rules must be supplied at runtime and routed to product stewardship or legal when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific relabel and artwork approval sequence
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources establish what evidence matters, but each tenant must supply the controlling document-control, artwork, and release sequence before a case-specific proceed or hold recommendation is safe.

notes: The research keeps the lane anchored to chemical-labeling process and evidence rather than generic compliance prose.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because market- and tenant-specific gaps are handled through explicit retrieval and escalation rules.
